meta.scenario: cut transition — Camelot distance 4, incompatible keys, T1 sparse outro

transition_arc: 3A (Bm) to 7B (F major) is a Camelot distance of 4 — harmonically incompatible. Any blend longer than 4 bars would produce obvious dissonance. T1 is in a very sparse outro section (rms < 0.15, only hi-hats remaining) — the near-silence masks the hard switch. A 4-bar quick fade_out is used instead of a full 16-bar blend. T2 enters at full volume with no stem filtering — the bass_swap is skipped because T1's bass is already gone in its sparse outro.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 bar 0: full groove, kick+bass active, RMS 0.52
T1 bar 72: section=OUTRO, drums=0.05, harmonic=0.0, rms=0.10 — only hi-hat flutter, bass completely gone
annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 0: full groove at entry, kick+bass+chords, RMS 0.58
annotated_actions.why: T1 at bar 72 is near-silent — the 4-bar quick fade is inaudible. No longer fade because the keys are incompatible (distance 4) — any harmonic bleed during a longer overlap would produce obvious dissonance. The near-silence at T1's outro makes the cut harmless.

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 0: full kick+bass+chords (F major), RMS 0.58 — enters at full volume
annotated_actions.why: No fade_in, no bass_swap needed. T1's bass is already gone. T2 enters at full mix volume immediately after T1's 4-bar fade. The key clash is minimized because T1 is already silent before T2 sounds.
